Off Calendar Mar-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Megan Cesare-Eastman, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Nancy Eastham, et al.

RULING:
Argued and Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The court already determined that, liberally construed, the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni read in conjunction with defendant's GO 129 interrogatory responses and Mr. Cole's declaration, creates a triable issue whether defendant's valves that Mr. Eastham was exposed to contained asbestos attributable to defendant. Dr. Bruch's declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Eastham's disease. Defendant's objections to Dr. Bruch's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
